name: Taw'a
title: Shelter of Solitude
shortDescription: A brave lady in Kufa who sheltered Muslim ibn Aqeel at the peak of the people's fear and oppression, immortalizing her name in history.
fullStory:
Taw'a was a noble and brave lady from Kufa. She was formerly a servant of Ash'ath ibn Qays, who was freed and married a man named Usayd.

When Ubaydullah ibn Ziyad dispersed the people of Kufa from around Muslim ibn Aqeel through threats and intimidation, the lonely ambassador of Imam Hussain (AS) was left solitary in the dark alleys of Kufa. Tired and thirsty, he leaned against the wall of a house.

Taw'a, who had come to the door waiting for her son, saw Muslim and brought him water. When she realized that he was Muslim ibn Aqeel, the envoy of Imam Hussain (AS) to whom all doors in Kufa had been closed, she sheltered him in her house with unparalleled bravery.

While the men of Kufa hid in their houses fearing for their lives, this brave woman accepted the risk of sheltering the government's most wanted man. Although Taw'a's unworthy son revealed Muslim's location the next morning, the name 'Taw'a' was recorded in history as a symbol of chivalry and insight.
quoteArabic: رَحِمَكِ اللَّهُ... أَنا مُسْلِمُ بْنُ عَقِيلٍ، كَذَّبَنِي هؤُلاءِ القَوْمُ وَغَرُّونِي...
quote: (Muslim said to her:) May God have mercy on you, I am Muslim ibn Aqeel. These people deceived me and lied...
